_schema: Vextreme file lattice — one entry per file, edges define reads/writes/loads/tests. Load this file first when arriving cold: it gives you the system topology without reading any implementation. Navigate laterally (adjacent nodes in changeMap) before diving into depth (file contents). This map is the write side; the LATTICE block inside each eligible file's own header is a generated read side (see lib/build-lattice-headers.js) — they cannot diverge because the file copy is regenerated from this one, not hand-maintained.
_usage: 1. Read this file before opening any lib/ file — understand what a file does and what it touches before reading it
2. When about to change something, look up the file's changeMap to find every adjacent node that must also be checked
3. Follow changeMap links recursively until you have visited every affected node (closed the circuit)

4. After adding or moving a file, add its node here, add it to the loadedBy/reads of its neighbors, then run: node lib/build-lattice-headers.js — this writes the matching in-file header for you (skip for .json nodes; JSON can't hold comments)

5. node lib/build-lattice-headers.js --check exits non-zero if any eligible file's header would change on regeneration — this is what CI runs (tests/11-lattice-headers.test.js) to catch drift between this JSON and the files it describes

6. Coverage is partial by design, not by accident: only files worth a lateral-navigation stop are nodes here. Most lib/ and widgets/ files are not yet mapped — see pe-009 in data/status/planned-enhancements.json for the expansion queue. Grep-ing for 'LATTICE:BEGIN' shows current coverage.

lib/compute-test-shards.js.role: self-scaling CI test-sharding — computes shard count and per-shard file assignment from the real current tests/*.test.js file count, no hand-picked number to forget updating
lib/compute-test-shards.js.context: Session 025 (continued) — Victor asked whether test sharding could be 'system expandable as a health and build process' rather than something a future instance eyeballs and manually flips on. computeShardCount() answers that: it's a pure function of the real current test-file count (TARGET_FILES_PER_SHARD, tuned so CI's fixed per-job overhead stays cheaper than actual test runtime until the suite has genuinely grown), recomputed by .github/workflows/test.yml's 'shards' job on every run. At today's ~28-file suite this resolves to 1 shard — the workflow runs as a single job, same as before this file existed. assignFilesToShard() is a deterministic round-robin partition (sorted file list, index modulo shard count) so the same inputs always produce the same split and every file lands in exactly one shard — verified by tests/29-compute-test-shards.test.js's partition-coverage tests. Deliberately uses file COUNT, not measured historical per-file duration, as the sharding signal — see the file's own docstring for why (this repo's tests are uniform and fast; a timing-log approach would need persisted CI-committed state to buy back precision this suite doesn't currently need).
reads: tests/*.test.js (file list only, via fs.readdirSync — not file contents)
writes: stdout (matrix JSON via `matrix` command, newline-separated file paths via `files` command)
loadedBy: .github/workflows/test.yml ('shards' job computes the matrix; 'test' job's matrix jobs compute their own file list)

TARGET_FILES_PER_SHARD or MAX_SHARDS cha: tests/29-compute-test-shards.test.js's integration test asserting the real suite currently resolves to 1 shard — a tighter target may make that assertion legitimately fail as the suite grows; that's the feature activating, not a bug, but confirm the shard count that produces before merging
.github/workflows/test.yml's 'all-shards-passed' aggregate job — stays a stable required-check name regardless of how many shards run, but worth re-confirming after a target change

lib/run-test-prereqs.js.role: runs the build scripts tests/helpers/prereqs.js declares as required before the test suite can read generated files — replaces a hardcoded run: line in .github/workflows/test.yml
lib/run-test-prereqs.js.context: Session 025 (continued) — before this file, the list of 'build scripts that must run before tests can pass' existed in two places that could drift: a literal `run:` line in test.yml, and prose in individual test files' PREREQUISITE docstrings (e.g. tests/04-build-output.test.js). This file and tests/helpers/prereqs.js collapse that to one array; both the CI step and any test file that wants to reference its own prerequisite point at the same source.
writes: whatever each script in PREREQUISITE_SCRIPTS writes (currently data/strings/compiled/*, pages/archives.html) — this file only orchestrates, it has no output of its own

a test file gains a new build-time prere: tests/helpers/prereqs.js's PREREQUISITE_SCRIPTS array — add it there, not to this file or the workflow directly
the new test file's own PREREQUISITE docstring — reference tests/helpers/prereqs.js rather than re-listing the scripts inline, so the two can't drift again

lib/vex-config.js.role: constants hub — pure declarations, no I/O
lib/vex-config.js.context: Every semantic string in the system (category names, language codes, feature IDs, scope names, CDN paths) lives here as a named constant. No build script or widget should hardcode 'demo', 'ja', 'common', etc. Changes here propagate to 9+ files — the widest fan-out of any single file.
loadedBy: tests/08-build-vextreme.test.js (FAB SYSTEM propagation tests — reads Feature.* constants directly)

scopeRelPath() logic changed: widgets/fab-lang.js (applies the same rule at runtime — must stay identical or fetches 404)

lib/build-index.js.role: content pipeline assembler — reads source data, writes the single pre-built index everything reads from
lib/build-index.js.context: The pipeline hub. Every page, every arc nav render, every God Script assembly starts from data/index.json which this file produces. If the index schema changes, 4+ consumers break. Run manually or via CI after editing nodes.json or arcs-v2.json. Session 022: nodes.json alone is no longer the complete node set this computes from — lib/auto-discover-nodes.js synthesizes a placeholder node for any pages/*.html file with no nodes.json entry, and those get concatenated in (allNodes) before buildSlugMap/buildArcMap/buildDepartmentMap run. findDuplicateSlugs still runs on raw nodes.json only — synthesized nodes can't collide with each other (one per real filename) or with curated ones (filtered out by definition).
reads: pages/*.html (via lib/auto-discover-nodes.js, for any slug with no nodes.json entry)
loadedBy: tests/08-build-vextreme.test.js (FAB SYSTEM propagation tests — calls buildViewmodel directly)

supportedLangs field: lib/build-vextreme.js (passes into every God Script as window.VEX_SUPPORTED_LANGS)
departmentMap / departmentMeta schema: data/departments.json (registry — add a department or workType here first)
findDuplicateSlugs / SLUG_DUPLICATE guar: docs/architecture/02-slug.md ('path is derived from slug' principle this guard protects)
auto-discovery logic changed (discoverOr: lib/auto-discover-nodes.js (the shared implementation both this file and lib/build-archives.js call)
lib/build-archives.js (calls the same function independently — see pe-011, tracked to collapse this someday)
tests/15-departments.test.js (its integration test replicates the allNodes computation)

lib/build-vextreme.js.role: God Script assembler — reads index + string bundles, writes one self-contained dist/ file per page
lib/build-vextreme.js.context: The write side of the page delivery pipeline. Inlines EN strings, bakes in viewmodel, includes feature widgets. Once a God Script is loaded by a page, it needs zero additional fetches for EN. Run after build-index.js and strings-compile.js. Session 025 FAB unification: spiral-fab and theme have real filenames now (vex-fab.js, fab-theme.js) after sitting reserved-but-unbuilt (filename: null) since Session 013; a new map feature (fab-map.js) was added alongside them. FEATURES object key order was changed so spiral-fab registers before lang/theme/map — that order is the script concatenation order, which is also the DOMContentLoaded firing order in the browser, and vex-fab.js's #vex-spiral-group container must exist before the other three widgets look for it on mount. Honest caveat found while building this: as of Session 025, ZERO live pages actually load a God Script (node lib/audit-pages.js reports 0 wired) — every real page still uses old standalone <script src=CDN> tags, so this feature (and the VEX_SUPPORTED_LANGS fix before it) is correct, tested infrastructure with no live effect until a page's script tags are actually switched over to its dist/ file. Not yet decided whether/when that wiring happens.
reads: lib/vextreme-index-v2.js (arc-nav feature, via srcDir: LIB_DIR in FEATURES registry)

FEATURES registry entry added: tests/08 (add coverage for new feature output)
arc-chunked bundling pilot (VEX_STRING_A: lib/build-arc-bundles.js (writes the bundle this global points at)
widgets/fab-lang.js (fetches it instead of per-scope fan-out when set)
lib/build-sw.js (precaches the arc bundle URLs for opted-in arcs)
scope bundle path convention: lib/strings-compile.js (must write to same path this reads from)
widgets/fab-lang.js (must fetch from same path at runtime)
dist/ output filenames: lib/build-sw.js (PRECACHE_URLS — sw.js must include new filenames)
pages/*.html (each page references its dist/ file by name)

lib/build-arc-bundles.js.role: arc-chunked language bundle assembler — the od-001/td-006 pilot, opt-in per arc
lib/build-arc-bundles.js.context: Resolves od-001 ('should the per-scope JA bundling strategy change before a 3rd language is added?') by proving the arc-chunked alternative td-006 names, scoped to one arc rather than migrating the whole site. Any arc with `bundlingStrategy: "arc-chunked"` in data/arcs-v2.json gets one data/strings/compiled/arcs/{arcId}.{lang}.json per language present — the merge of every member slug's scope bundles, done once at build time instead of an N-way runtime fetch. Arcs that don't opt in are completely unaffected. Today's only opted-in arc is victor_dossier (Session 025).
writes: data/strings/compiled/arcs/{arcId}.{lang}.json (one per opted-in arc per language present)
data/strings/compiled/arcs/index.json (which arcs are chunked, their langs, key counts)

a second arc opts into bundlingStrategy:: nothing code-side — this script already generalizes; only the pilot's scope is one arc, not the mechanism
data/strings/compiled/arcs/{arcId}.{lang: widgets/fab-lang.js (fetches this exact path when window.VEX_STRING_ARC_BUNDLE is set)
lib/build-sw.js (precaches this exact path for opted-in arcs)

lib/strings-compile.js.role: strings pipeline compiler — reads source JSON, writes compiled bundles and manifest
lib/strings-compile.js.context: The write side of the i18n pipeline. Every string key, every language bundle, every stale-detection hash is produced here. Run after editing any file in data/strings/source/. The scope bundle path rule here MUST match the runtime fetch rule in widgets/fab-lang.js.
loadedBy: lib/build-vextreme.js (loads its output data/strings/compiled/scopes bundles for God Script assembly)
widgets/fab-lang.js (fetches its output data/strings/compiled/scopes bundles at runtime for JA)
lib/strings-check.js (reads its output data/strings/compiled/manifest.json for stale detection)

scopeRelPath() path rule: widgets/fab-lang.js (applies the same rule at runtime — must stay identical)

lib/vextreme-index-v2.js.role: browser arc nav runtime — reads index.json at load time, renders prev/next/position into #arcNavMount
lib/vextreme-index-v2.js.context: THE arc nav widget for the v2 architecture. Supersedes lib/arc-nav.js (v1, Squarespace-era, reads VEXTREME_ARCS). Inlined into God Scripts via the arc-nav feature in the FEATURES registry. Also loads standalone on non-God-Script pages via shell.js.
reads: window.VEX_STRINGS_EN (God Script fast path — already inlined by build-vextreme.js)
testedBy: tests/03-browser-nav.test.js (data logic; no browser render tests yet)

urlFromSlug() URL construction: pages/*.html filenames (must match the URLs this generates)
#arcNavMount selector: every page HTML that uses arc-nav must have <div id="arcNavMount">

lib/arc-nav.js.role: v1 Squarespace-era arc nav — DO NOT USE for God Script pages
lib/arc-nav.js.context: The original arc nav widget, designed for squarespace-injection.html. Reads from window.VEXTREME_ARCS (old arcs.json format), not index.json. Kept for reference and the Squarespace deployment path. For all GitHub Pages / God Script pages, use lib/vextreme-index-v2.js instead.
reads: window.PAGE_ARCS (set per-page by the embedding loader)
loadedBy: lib/vextreme.js (v1 runtime — loadScript of /lib/arc-nav.js; squarespace-injection.html only loads vextreme.js)

data/nodes.json.role: content node source — the authoritative list of all content slugs
data/nodes.json.context: Every piece of content is a node here. The slug is the system's only identifier — arc membership, string scope keys, HTML filenames, and God Script names all derive from it. This is the write side; data/index.json is the read side.
loadedBy: lib/build-index.js (primary consumer — reads on every build)

slug added: data/arcs-v2.json (add to arc membership if it belongs to an arc)
data/strings/source/ (create scope file for the page's strings)
data/viewmodels.json (add override if not production/page defaults)
slug renamed: see pe-006: lib/rename-slug.js tooling planned but not yet built

data/arcs-v2.json.role: arc structure source — defines arc membership, ordering, and metadata
data/arcs-v2.json.context: Arcs are the navigation layer: ordered groups of content nodes with display metadata. The v2 format uses slug-keyed entries (not URL-keyed). build-index.js reads this alongside nodes.json to produce arcMap and arcMeta in index.json.

arc entry added: data/nodes.json (all slugs in the arc must exist there)

data/departments.json.role: department registry source — production-domain grouping, orthogonal to arcs (narrative) and blueprint.json categories (publish lifecycle)
data/departments.json.context: A node with no department/workType field defaults to whichever entry here has default:true (and that department's 'default' workType). Currently two departments: rd (default) and media (workTypes: reviews, record-transcripts). This is the write side; data/index.json's departmentMap/departmentMeta is the read side lib/build-ecosystem-hub.js actually renders from.

department or workType added: data/nodes.json (nodes opting into the new department/workType — department field, workType field)
tests/15-departments.test.js (add coverage for the new entry if it changes default-fallback behavior)
default:true department changed: every node.json entry with no explicit department field now resolves to the new default — verify that's intended before changing

data/council-kernel.json.role: hand-transcribed structured extract of pages/org-blueprint.html's roster and patterns — the 'runnable kernel' that document names as its own unfinished ambition
data/council-kernel.json.context: Session 022: Victor shared external design-document URLs and asked whether one AI instance could hold internal multi-lens 'council' awareness instead of coordinating across multiple instances. pages/org-blueprint.html (already in this repo, previously uncurated) already describes exactly this — a Scanner that fires multiple internal 'faculty' perspectives in parallel before committing to a response. This file transcribes that roster/pattern into queryable form, same as data/departments.json is hand-authored rather than derived. See docs/architecture/14-council-model.md for the honest assessment of what this can and can't be — a single instance's structured self-check, not multiple independent agents.
loadedBy: pages/ecosystem-hub.html (live fetch, browser — the Council Lenses panel, added Session 022 same day as this note)
lib/build-roles.js (compiles this roster + data/status/*.json's lens fields into data/roles.json)

a role added or removed: tests/19-council-kernel.test.js's caughtBy-reference-integrity check must still pass
docs/architecture/14-council-model.md if the change affects the honest-limits framing
lib/build-ecosystem-hub.js renderLenses() reads role.id/holds/failsAs/caughtBy directly — a shape change breaks it silently unless tests/19 or tests/12 catch it
lib/build-roles.js buildRolesManifest() reads the same fields, plus decisionTriangle/connectionArchitecture — a shape change breaks it silently unless tests/19 or tests/20 catch it
pages/org-blueprint.html's source conten: this file was hand-transcribed at a point in time (Draft v0.5) — re-check it still matches if the source page is ever revised

data/viewmodels.json.role: per-slug viewmodel overrides — demo/specimen pages that aren't content nodes
data/viewmodels.json.context: Content nodes in nodes.json get default viewmodels (production/page template). This file holds overrides for demo and specimen pages that have different categories, templates, or feature sets. Also used to add non-node slugs (demo pages, specimens) to the God Script assembly pipeline.

slug entry added/changed: lib/build-vextreme.js (rebuild God Script for that slug)
data/strings/source/ (must have compiled scope bundle for assembly to succeed)

data/index.json.role: pre-built read index — the single artifact every page and build script reads from
data/index.json.context: Generated by lib/build-index.js. Never edit directly. Contains slugMap (slug → viewmodel + arcKeys + nav data), arcMap (arc → sections → slug lists), arcMeta (arc → display metadata), departmentMap (department → workType → [{slug, title, ported}]), departmentMeta (department → display labels), supportedLangs, nodeCount, arcCount, builtAt. Everything downstream reads this; nothing downstream reads nodes.json, arcs-v2.json, or departments.json directly.
loadedBy: lib/vextreme-index-v2.js (browser fetches via CDN, caches in localStorage)

schema changes: lib/build-index.js (producer — update to emit new schema)

widgets/vex-fab.js.role: spiral FAB — trigger button + shared expandable group container that other orb widgets mount into
widgets/vex-fab.js.context: Session 025 FAB unification: activates the spiral-fab Feature slot reserved-but-unbuilt (filename: null) since Session 013 — Victor asked for one expandable FAB framework instead of one-off widgets, so a new orb is a small widget registration, not a change to this file. Renders a trigger button (#vex-spiral-trigger, 🌀) that toggles a group container (#vex-spiral-group); widgets/fab-theme.js, widgets/fab-map.js, and widgets/fab-lang.js each look up that container on their OWN DOMContentLoaded handler and append their orb into it if present, falling back to a standalone top-level mount if this widget isn't active on the page. This file does not need to change when an orb is added or removed — that is the whole point of the split. MUST be listed before lang/theme/map in lib/build-vextreme.js's FEATURES registry so its DOMContentLoaded handler (which creates #vex-spiral-group) fires before theirs (handlers fire in registration order = script concatenation order).
loadedBy: lib/build-vextreme.js (inlined as spiral-fab feature in God Scripts, must be first among the FAB-group features)

widgets/fab-theme.js.role: dark/light theme toggle orb — sets document.documentElement's data-theme attribute, persists to localStorage
widgets/fab-theme.js.context: Session 025 FAB unification: activates the theme Feature slot reserved-but-unbuilt (filename: null) since Session 013. Ships the real toggle MECHANISM only — it does not add [data-theme="dark"] CSS to the ~100+ content pages that each hand-author their own inline :root token block rather than sharing styles/design-system.css, so toggling currently has no visible effect on most pages. A page that adds its own dark CSS later needs zero JS changes to pick this up. Mounts into #vex-spiral-group (widgets/vex-fab.js) when present, else falls back to a standalone top-level fixed-position button.
loadedBy: lib/build-vextreme.js (inlined as theme feature in God Scripts)

per-page dark CSS added to any page: no JS change needed here — the mechanism already sets data-theme="dark"/"light" on <html>; a page's own CSS keys off that attribute

widgets/fab-map.js.context: Session 025 FAB unification: activates the new map Feature slot. Distinct from widgets/fab-demo.js's older 'architecture demo' orb (pages/vextreme-demo.html) — Victor: the demo page concept is becoming outdated as the real production system (the terrain map itself) demonstrates the architecture better than a condensed stand-in page. Mounts into #vex-spiral-group (widgets/vex-fab.js) when present, else falls back to a standalone top-level fixed-position button.
loadedBy: lib/build-vextreme.js (inlined as map feature in God Scripts)

widgets/fab-lang.js.role: browser language switcher FAB — fetches JA/ZH scope or arc bundles, applies [data-i18n] swaps
widgets/fab-lang.js.context: The runtime i18n engine. On language switch, constructs scope bundle URLs using the same scopeRelPath rule as strings-compile.js (build time) — these MUST stay in sync. Session 025 correction: fetched strings are held only in the in-memory _langStrings variable, not cached in localStorage — only the selected language preference (LS_LANG) persists there; a fresh fetch happens on every switch. Reads VEX_STRING_SCOPES, VEX_STRING_CATEGORY from window (set by God Script). Session 025 also added an arc-chunked bundle path (od-001/td-006 pilot): if window.VEX_STRING_ARC_BUNDLE is set, fetches the one merged arc bundle instead of the per-scope fan-out below. Session 025 (continued) — real bug found: lib/build-vextreme.js has baked window.VEX_SUPPORTED_LANGS into every God Script since it was introduced (verified: dossier's God Script correctly emits ["en","ja","zh"]), but loadSupportedLangs() here never read it — it went straight to a localStorage blob written by a DIFFERENT widget (vextreme-index-v2.js's LS_DATA cache) or a live index.json fetch, neither of which is guaranteed to reflect what THIS page's God Script actually has (a browser that cached LS_DATA before a language was added never sees the addition until that shared key happens to be overwritten). This is the same shape as PR #76's ARC_ORDER gap — real data existing while a separate consumption path silently doesn't know about it. loadSupportedLangs() now checks window.VEX_SUPPORTED_LANGS first, matching the existing VEX_STRINGS_EN fast-path pattern. Session 025 (continued, again) — Victor reported the fix still wasn't visible on the live dossier page after that PR merged. Root cause was a second, independent bug: pages/victor-methodology-presentation.html loads this file directly via a jsdelivr CDN script tag (@main, no version query string), not via a God Script — so the fix landing on GitHub's main branch doesn't mean the CDN's cached copy of THIS FILE updates; jsdelivr caches branch-alias URLs for a real, non-trivial TTL independent of new commits. Separately, this file's own VERSION constant (used only to cache-bust the internal index.json fetch, a different cache key from the script-tag URL above) had never been bumped since introduction, so even a fresh copy of this file would keep re-fetching a stale cached index.json indefinitely. Fixed the second one (VERSION bumped 1.1.0 -> 1.2.0). The first (script-tag-level CDN staleness) cannot be fixed from inside this file — it needs a manual purge (https://purge.jsdelivr.net/gh/vgong24/vextreme@main/widgets/fab-lang.js) or jsdelivr's own TTL to lapse; the sandbox this fix was authored in cannot reach jsdelivr's domain to trigger that purge itself (network policy blocks it), so a human has to do it. widgets/lang-fab.js (the pre-rename legacy duplicate, loaded directly by pages/claude-answers-the-doubt.html) has the same never-bumped-VERSION issue and does NOT have the VEX_SUPPORTED_LANGS fix at all — known, not yet fixed, out of scope for this pass.
reads: window.VEX_STRING_ARC_BUNDLE (arc-chunked bundling pilot, checked before the scope path)
localStorage (LS_LANG — the selected language preference only, not fetched string content)
DOM: #vex-spiral-group (widgets/vex-fab.js — Session 025 FAB unification, nests its orb there if present)
loadedBy: lib/build-vextreme.js (inlined as lang feature in God Scripts)
legacy pages via direct CDN script tag (pre-God-Script pattern)
testedBy: tests/08-build-vextreme.test.js (structural guard: loadSupportedLangs() must read window.VEX_SUPPORTED_LANGS)

localStorage key format: any future offline FAB that reads cache state (pe-008)
VEX_* global names read: lib/build-vextreme.js assembleGodScript() (must emit matching global names)

widgets/sw-register.js.role: Service Worker registration — activates offline caching on first page load
widgets/sw-register.js.context: Core module: default: true in the FEATURES registry, inlined into every God Script. Registers sw.js. Once active, the SW pre-caches all God Scripts + core assets at install time — subsequent page loads make zero network requests for HTML or JS. Without this, the offline/caching pyramid is inactivated.
loadedBy: lib/build-vextreme.js (inlined as core module in every God Script, default: true)
testedBy: tests/09-build-sw.test.js (tests sw.js generation, not this file directly)

SW registration scope or path: sw.js (must exist at the registered path)

sw.js.role: Service Worker — pre-caches all God Scripts and core assets at install
sw.js.context: Generated by lib/build-sw.js. Pre-caches every dist/vextreme-{slug}.js file plus core CSS/JSON assets. After install, page visits cost zero network requests for cached assets. Stale SW caches are cleaned up on activate. Never edit directly — regenerate with node lib/build-sw.js.
loadedBy: widgets/sw-register.js (registers this file in every God Script page)

PRECACHE_URLS list: lib/build-sw.js (generates this list from dist/ filenames)
run: node lib/build-sw.js after adding any new dist/ file

lib/build-sw.js.role: Service Worker generator — reads dist/ filenames + arc-chunked bundle index, writes sw.js
lib/build-sw.js.context: Scans dist/ for God Script filenames and bakes them into the SW PRECACHE_URLS list. Run after build-vextreme.js adds new dist/ files. The generated sw.js is committed — the SW cache version is pinned to the git commit hash at generation time. Session 025 added precaching for lib/build-arc-bundles.js's opted-in arc bundles (od-001/td-006 pilot) — bounded to (opted-in arcs x their languages), not every scope of every page.

CORE_ASSETS list: styles/design-system.css, styles/arc-nav.css, data/index.json (must exist at these paths)
cache name format: sw.js activate handler (cleans caches by name pattern)
data/strings/compiled/arcs/index.json sh: collectArcBundleUrls() here — reads entry.langs keys directly

lib/build-lattice-headers.js.role: LATTICE header generator — reads this JSON, writes the matching in-file header into each eligible node's own file
lib/build-lattice-headers.js.context: This file exists because lattice-map.json and in-file LATTICE headers used to be two hand-maintained copies of the same facts, which drifted the moment one was edited without the other. This script makes the JSON the write side and the in-file block the generated read side — the same CQRS shape used everywhere else in this repo (nodes.json → index.json, strings/source → strings/compiled). Never hand-edit text between LATTICE:BEGIN/LATTICE:END markers in any file; edit this JSON and run the script.
writes: LATTICE:BEGIN..LATTICE:END block in every eligible node's own file

node added/removed/edited in this JSON: run: node lib/build-lattice-headers.js to regenerate the affected file's header
tests/11 --check enforces this in CI — a PR with JSON changes but no regenerated header fails the drift check
LATTICE:BEGIN/LATTICE:END marker text ch: every file already carrying markers (grep for LATTICE:BEGIN) — old marker text will no longer be found, orphaning that block
comment-prefix detection logic changed: any node whose target file uses line-comment style instead of block-comment style (e.g. lib/strings-compile.js uses // ) — verify both styles still round-trip

lib/auto-discover-nodes.js.role: synthesizes a node-shaped object for any pages/*.html file with no data/nodes.json entry, so adding a page alone is enough to make it visible
lib/auto-discover-nodes.js.context: Built in response to a real gap: pages merged directly (not through this repo's usual node-first flow) sat completely invisible in data/index.json and pages/archives.html except for an easy-to-miss PR-comment warning (lib/check-key-alignment.js's orphan-page check). discoverOrphanNodes() is called independently by both lib/build-index.js and lib/build-archives.js (see pe-011 — ideally only one of them would need to, if build-archives.js consumed index.json instead of re-deriving from nodes.json). Synthesized nodes are never written back to data/nodes.json — nodes.json stays hand-curated, single source of truth; a real nodes.json entry for the same slug always takes precedence once one exists. A page can declare its own department/workType via <meta name="vex:department"> / <meta name="vex:workType"> tags, entirely without touching JSON; absent that, it falls back to whichever department in data/departments.json is marked default:true.
writes: nothing — pure in-memory synthesis, never persisted to data/nodes.json
loadedBy: lib/build-archives.js (feeds the Unsorted section — see pe-011 for why this is two call sites, not one)

tests/16, tests/15 (integration test replicates this shape)

lib/audit-pages.js.role: page wiring auditor — read-only report of which pages/*.html are wired to a God Script, blocked, or intentionally skipped
lib/audit-pages.js.context: The canonical way to answer 'what's the current wiring status' without manually reading every page. CLAUDE.md points here instead of describing wiring status in prose, since prose goes stale and this doesn't — it reads dist/ and pages/ directly on every run. SKIP_PAGES and BLOCKERS are the only hand-maintained parts; both are dated the moment a page's status actually changes.
reads: pages/*.html (checks for a dist/vextreme-{slug}.js script src)
loadedBy: CLAUDE.md (points here as the wiring-status source of truth)
lib/check-key-alignment.js (imports SKIP_PAGES + getPageSlugs — reuses the skip list instead of duplicating it)
lib/build-terrain-map.js (getPageSlugs — real page files vs. curated nodes.json entries, for the content/arc layer's uncurated column)

a page becomes wired/unwired: SKIP_PAGES or BLOCKERS entry in this file may need updating if the reason changes
CLAUDE.md 'Current blockers' section (prose summary — keep loosely in sync)
a new generated page added (own loader,: SKIP_PAGES entry — otherwise it reports as a false blocker
lib/check-key-alignment.js's orphan-page check also reads SKIP_PAGES — a page that's a false positive there is fixed the same way
SKIP_PAGES / getPageSlugs export shape c: lib/check-key-alignment.js (destructures both by name — a rename here breaks that require)

lib/check-key-alignment.js.role: non-blocking CI check — compares slug/arc keys across nodes.json, arcs-v2.json, index.json, cross-checks pages/ and wip/, and reports drift
lib/check-key-alignment.js.context: JSON is string-based; a slug or arc key can drift between the three files silently (node added but index.json not rebuilt, arc renamed but index stale). This check catches that class of bug with a PR comment, without blocking merge — informational only. Complements lib/audit-pages.js (page wiring) and lib/build-lattice-headers.js --check (header drift) as the third 'silent drift' detector in the repo. Session 022 added two more checks in the same spirit: orphan pages (a pages/*.html file with no nodes.json entry — excludes SKIP_PAGES and data/viewmodels.json-registered dev/demo pages, both legitimate non-node pages) and wip/ slug collisions (a wip/*.json file's optional _meta.slug already exists as a real pages/*.html file). A wip/ slug matching only a nodes.json placeholder with no page yet is deliberately NOT flagged — that's the expected linkage (the placeholder exists precisely to reserve that wip file's destination), not a conflict. Session 022 continued: Victor added wip/victor-methodology-presentation.html directly and found zero automated mapping — this file's wip/ scan only ever read *.json's _meta.slug, never raw *.html. scanWipHtmlDrafts() closes that: every wip/*.html file's filename becomes its working slug (derived, not declared), scraped the same way lib/auto-discover-nodes.js's discoverWipDrafts scrapes a title, and merged into the same collision/duplicate-intent checks as json-declared intents — a draft's filename colliding with a real page is exactly as real a mistake either way. Session 025 (continued): a real gap found the hard way — the victor_dossier arc existed in arcs-v2.json and even reached index.json cleanly, but its one real page was unreachable from Archives for a full session because three separate hand-maintained rendering constants (lib/build-archives.js's ARC_ORDER and ARC_KEY_MAP, lib/build-index-page.js's own separate priority-1-only ARC_ORDER) each independently needed the same arc key added and none of them were. findArcsUnwiredInRendering() closes that: scans the two files' source TEXT via regex (not require(), since both files execute their build as a side effect at require-time with no require.main guard) and reports any arc present in arcs-v2.json but absent from any of the three constants. This is the same shape of gap as PR #74's SKIP_PAGES dual-meaning fix — a piece of real data agreeing with itself while a separate rendering list silently doesn't know about it — but a different list, a different silent-failure mode, caught only because Victor happened to look for the page and not find it.
reads: wip/*.html (filename-derived slug + title, via lib/auto-discover-nodes.js discoverWipDrafts)
lib/build-archives.js (source text — ARC_ORDER, ARC_KEY_MAP, regex-scanned not required)
lib/build-index-page.js (source text — its own separate ARC_ORDER, regex-scanned not required)
writes: stdout (JSON report with --json flag); PR comment via .github/workflows/key-alignment.yml
loadedBy: lib/apply-content-intents.js (validates department/arcKey against the same maps)

report schema changed: .github/workflows/key-alignment.yml (formats the PR comment table from this shape)
lib/build-status.js (buildContentIntegrityNotices reads report.pages.orphans, report.wip.collisions, report.wip.duplicateIntents, report.wip.htmlDrafts)
wip/ collision rule changed (what counts: tests/08 (WIP-COLLISION tests — especially the placeholder-is-not-a-collision case)
data/departments.json's _comment (documents the convention this check enforces)
ARC_ORDER/ARC_KEY_MAP renamed or reshape: extractQuotedListFromSource()/extractObjectKeysFromSource() here — regex against exact const name and array/object literal syntax, not an AST parse; a renamed constant or a non-literal (e.g. built via .concat()) silently stops being detected rather than erroring

lib/detect-wip-promotions.js.role: detects a wip/*.html → pages/*.html move within a PR's diff, so a promotion shows up as a positive PR notice instead of silence or a false 'broken reference'
lib/detect-wip-promotions.js.context: The second half of Victor's stated intent (Session 022 continued): once a wip/*.html draft has an initial mapping (lib/auto-discover-nodes.js discoverWipDrafts / lib/check-key-alignment.js scanWipHtmlDrafts), moving it to pages/{same-slug}.html later should read as a reconciliation, not an error. Deliberately reuses git's own similarity-based rename detection (git diff --name-status -M) rather than persisting any cross-build state to compare against — nothing tracks 'this slug used to be in wip/', so there is nothing to go stale or conflict when the file moves; the draft simply stops appearing in scanWipHtmlDrafts's next-build output, which is correct behavior by construction, not a special case handled here. This file only adds the positive notice on top of that already-correct baseline.
reads: git diff --name-status -M output between two refs (wip/ and pages/ paths only)
writes: stdout (JSON with --json flag); PR comment via .github/workflows/key-alignment.yml

promotion shape changed (fields added/re: .github/workflows/key-alignment.yml (formats the PR comment section from this shape)
similarity threshold changed: .github/workflows/key-alignment.yml (the -M flag passed to git diff must match what this file's doc comment claims)

lib/build-status.js.role: system health manifest assembler — reads hand-authored status/*.json plus the strings manifest and key-alignment report, writes data/status.json
lib/build-status.js.context: The write side of the 'what's still open' picture: six notice categories (translation debt and contentIntegrity auto-derived, techDebt/enhancements/assumptions/openDiscussions hand-authored) roll up into one artifact pages/ecosystem-hub.html fetches live. Also computes lattice coverage (mapped nodes vs. total eligible .js files in lib/+widgets/) into _meta.lattice, so the hub can show it without re-deriving the eligibility rule. openDiscussions (added Session 015) is deliberately separate from techDebt/enhancements — see data/status/open-discussions.json's _comment for why. contentIntegrity (Session 022) is auto-generated like translation, not hand-authored like the other three. Also Session 022: this generator had been run and committed by hand every session with no CI step actually calling it — now wired into .github/workflows/build-index.yml alongside lib/build-ecosystem-hub.js and the new lib/build-lessons.js. Later, Session 022: _meta.generated (wall-clock timestamp) and _meta.commit (git SHA at build time) removed entirely — neither was consumed anywhere, and both guaranteed a manufactured merge conflict any time two branches independently regenerated this file from identical source data (real incident, PR #50). Output is now content-deterministic: identical input always produces a byte-identical file. See config/lessons/generated-artifacts-must-be-content-deterministic.json.
reads: lib/ and widgets/ directory listings (.js files, for coverage denominator)
lib/check-key-alignment.js (checkKeyAlignment() — orphan pages, wip collisions/duplicate intents)
loadedBy: pages/ecosystem-hub.html (live fetch of its output data/status.json)

lattice coverage eligibility rule change: lib/build-lattice-headers.js isEligibleNode() (must stay identical or the two coverage numbers disagree)
check-key-alignment.js report shape chan: buildContentIntegrityNotices() here reads report.pages.orphans / report.wip.collisions / report.wip.duplicateIntents / report.wip.htmlDrafts directly — a shape change there breaks this silently unless tests/10, tests/08, or tests/21 catch it
any new _meta field being considered: must not vary run-to-run for identical source data (no wall-clock timestamp, no git commit SHA) — see config/lessons/generated-artifacts-must-be-content-deterministic.json for why; if a field genuinely needs to reflect build time (like data/index.json's builtAt), that's a deliberate, different tradeoff, not the default

lib/apply-content-intents.js.role: applies config/content-intents.json's pending entries — mutates pages/*.html meta tags and data/arcs-v2.json's auto-managed sections, then runs the existing sanity-check pipeline
lib/apply-content-intents.js.context: Session 022: generalizes the existing vex:department/vex:workType meta-tag pattern (declare intent on the page, let a build step read it) to arc membership too. Rather than inventing a new order:"auto" mode in arcs-v2.json's schema, each arc gets one ordinary explicit section with a reserved label (AUTO_SECTION_LABEL) that this tool alone manages — lib/build-archives.js's getArcSlugs() and lib/build-index.js's buildArcMap() need zero changes, since a flat explicit slugs array is exactly what they already render. Re-declaring a slug's arcKey moves it (removes from every other arc's auto section first) rather than duplicating it — the 'repo's own self-awareness of its prior mappings' Victor asked for. Never touches a hand-curated (non-auto-label) section — arc curation stays a human act. validateIntent() catches two silent-failure modes found by actually running this against real content: an unregistered department (buildDepartmentMap groups by any string with no validation, but build-ecosystem-hub.js's renderDepartments() only iterates departmentMeta's registered keys, so unregistered content is present in index.json but rendered nowhere) and a nonexistent arcKey (applyArcPlacement would otherwise throw mid-loop, aborting every remaining intent in the same run). Also: upsertMetaTag() must prepend rather than insert-before-</head> for pages with no <head> at all — several real candidate pages (about-me.html, connect.html) are Squarespace paste-block fragments with no HTML document wrapper; found by running this tool against them, not anticipated in advance. After applying, shells out to lib/build-index.js (the real BLOCK gate for duplicate slugs) then calls lib/check-key-alignment.js's checkKeyAlignment() directly as the sanity-check report, same as every other content change in this repo is verified against.
reads: pages/*.html (only the slugs named by pending intents)
writes: pages/*.html (meta tag upserts, only for slugs named by pending intents)

vex:department / vex:workType meta tag c: lib/auto-discover-nodes.js parsePageMeta() (must stay compatible with what this file writes)
AUTO_SECTION_LABEL or arc-placement rule: lib/build-archives.js getArcSlugs() / lib/build-index.js buildArcMap() — both must keep treating an auto-managed section as an ordinary explicit section (no special-casing needed as long as the label doesn't collide with a real curated label)

lib/build-lessons.js.role: compiles config/lessons/*.json into data/lessons.json — the read-side manifest the Ecosystem Hub fetches
lib/build-lessons.js.context: config/lessons/*.json has been the write-side home for distilled architectural/process lessons since Session 010 (see docs/architecture/08-continuity.md's collaboration-documents table), but nothing compiled those files into anything pages/ecosystem-hub.html — the one dashboard meant to answer 'what does this system currently know' — could read. A lesson was discoverable (git log --grep, grepping the directory, or the partial hand-authored decision cards in pages/specimen-architectural-wisdoms.html) but not connected to the generated read side, the same shape of gap lib/auto-discover-nodes.js closed for pages/ in the same session. Handles two lesson-file schema generations: newer files use sessions: [...], older ones (Session 010-011) use pr/session singular fields — both normalize to one sessions array in the output.
reads: config/lessons/*.json (every lesson file, both schema generations)
loadedBy: pages/ecosystem-hub.html (live fetch of the compiled output)

a new lesson file added to config/lesson: no action needed — this script picks up every *.json file in the directory automatically on its next run (manual or CI)
lesson file schema changed (new required: buildLessonsManifest() in this file (the field mapping)

lib/build-ecosystem-hub.js.role: generates pages/ecosystem-hub.html — the admin+AI-facing system state dashboard
lib/build-ecosystem-hub.js.context: Static chrome generated at build time; all data (index.json, status.json, status/narrative.json, lessons.json) is fetched live in the browser at render time, so the page stays current between rebuilds without needing this generator re-run for data-only changes. Session 015 rewrote the CSS after finding it referenced --stone-950/--font-mono tokens that styles/design-system.css never defines — text rendered near-invisible. Now uses only the site's real tokens (--stone, --muted, --border, --cream, --ember, --mono), same as lib/build-demo.js. Session 022 found this file (and lib/build-status.js) had been generated and committed every session but never actually wired into .github/workflows/build-index.yml — both were regenerated by hand each time, an unenforced manual ritual with no CI backstop if a session forgot. Now wired into the workflow alongside the new lib/build-lessons.js, so a Content Map / System Health / Lessons Learned change lands in the committed HTML automatically, same as every other generated artifact in this pipeline.

CSS custom property used in the inline <: styles/design-system.css — the property must actually be defined there (--stone, --muted, --border, --cream, --ember, --ember-bg, --mono, --serif, --sans); anything else silently renders with no fallback
CATEGORY_ORDER / CATEGORY_ICONS arrays in this file's generated JS
lib/build-status.js (the category must exist in the rollup first)
data/status/narrative.json schema change: renderNarrative() in this file's generated JS

data/lessons.json schema changed: renderLessons() in this file's generated JS
data/council-kernel.json schema changed: renderLenses() in this file's generated JS

lib/build-roles.js.role: compiles data/council-kernel.json's roster + data/status/*.json's lens fields into data/roles.json — the traceable roles/contributions registry
lib/build-roles.js.context: Session 022's Council Lenses panel gave each role a name and a place to be looked up, but named nothing about which real decisions had actually invoked a role's judgment — Victor's follow-up ask named this directly: roles should be 'positioned' with 'traceability', not randomly placed. This script closes that gap by cross-referencing every data/status/*.json item's optional lens field against the kernel roster, so a role's page shows either real linked contributions or an explicit zero, never silence. position is deliberately the same string for every role today ('org-wide — The Council') since only one council exists in this repo — claiming a per-department position would overclaim a structure (per-department Bridge Councils) that doesn't exist yet. channels pass connectionArchitecture through with one added field, manifestation, naming what (if anything) currently instantiates that channel for real rather than only describing it in the abstract.
loadedBy: pages/roles-index.html (live fetch of its output data/roles.json — via lib/build-roles-page.js)

an item gets (or loses) a lens field in: no action needed — this script picks it up automatically on its next run
data/council-kernel.json roster/decision: lib/build-roles-page.js renderRoles()/renderChannels() (reads the same fields back out)

lib/build-roles-page.js.role: generates pages/roles-index.html — the traceable roles/contributions dashboard
lib/build-roles-page.js.context: Static chrome generated at build time; data/roles.json is fetched live in the browser at render time, same pattern as lib/build-ecosystem-hub.js. Deliberately a separate page rather than a bigger Council Lenses panel on the hub — Victor asked for 'an index json and webpage', and a role's full contribution list doesn't fit the hub's compact one-line-per-item health-panel style without crowding it. Linked from the hub's Council Lenses section ('Full roles & contributions →') rather than duplicated there.

data/roles.json schema changed: renderRoles() / renderChannels() in this file's generated JS
CSS custom property used in the inline <: styles/design-system.css — the property must actually be defined there (--stone, --muted, --border, --cream, --ember, --ember-bg, --mono, --serif, --sans); anything else silently renders with no fallback

lib/build-terrain-map.js.role: terrain-map write side — computes layout, health, edges, and real screenshots into one deterministic JSON
lib/build-terrain-map.js.context: Session 025 generalized the terrain-map pilot (an Artifact over 14 hand-picked nodes shown to Victor as a proof of concept) into a real, self-updating feature. Same CQRS split as data/status.json -> pages/ecosystem-hub.html: this script is the write side, pages/terrain-map.html is hand-authored once and live-fetches its output. Adding a lattice node, a page, or a screenshot never requires touching the HTML — only rerunning this generator, wired into CI the same as every other dashboard's data source. Session 025 (continued) replaced the folder-grid layout with a lifecycle-stage layout (source -> generate -> utilities -> check -> output -> runtime) and added two role-lens tags per node (engineerFocus / auditorFocus), after a round of Artifact POCs tested a fractal zoom-level UI (system -> stage -> node, each level snapping to its own full framing on entry, exiting always stepping back one level) against this exact shape. The stage/lens rules are naming-pattern heuristics over real fields, explicitly not a designed taxonomy — each node's lens tags render visibly in the UI so the heuristic can be challenged, not hidden behind a silent classification. Session 025 (continued) again: added computeExecutionOrder (real within-stage ordering sourced from .github/workflows/build-index.yml's literal step sequence and lib/session-bootstrap.js's own check order, not alphabetical) and per-node auditorRank (an alternate within-stage ordering by worst-status-first — the read side swaps to it, recomputing position, only while the auditor lens is active). Also added a second, parallel content layer (layoutArcs/buildContentPages -> the `arcs`/`pages` output fields): the same column-per-group technique applied to data/nodes.json grouped by arc instead of lifecycle stage, ordered within an arc by each node's own real `id` field (curation order) rather than alphabetically. A curated node with no matching pages/*.html file is marked live:false, status:'critical' rather than implied to be a real page — most of this repo's 91 curated nodes are unported v1 content, and that is real, common state to show, not an edge case to hide. A real pages/*.html file with no nodes.json entry lands in a distinct 'uncurated' arc column instead of being silently absent.
reads: data/nodes.json (real page titles/arcKeys, for both the screens layer and the arcs/pages content layer)
data/arcs-v2.json (real arc priority/title, for the arcs/pages content layer's column order and labels)
lib/audit-pages.js (getPageSlugs — real page files vs. curated nodes.json entries, for the content layer's uncurated column and each page's live flag)
docs/screenshots/*.png (directory listing — which pages have a real per-language screenshot)

docs/lattice-map.json schema changed (re: computeEdges() / findDebtReferences() here read those fields directly
data/terrain-map.json schema changed (no: pages/terrain-map.html (the only consumer — live-fetches this exact shape, including the CONTENT world's arcs/pages fields)
data/arcs-v2.json's priority field or ke: layoutArcs() here sorts arc columns by priority then key — a differently-shaped priority value silently reorders or misgroups the content layer
screenshot filename convention ({slug}-{: findScreenshots() here — the regex encodes that exact shape

pages/terrain-map.html.role: the terrain map's read side — a navigable health/dependency atlas, live-fetches data/terrain-map.json
pages/terrain-map.html.context: Hand-authored once, like pages/ecosystem-hub.html's own interaction chrome; unlike that file, this one is not itself regenerated by a build script — only its data source (data/terrain-map.json) is. Session 025 (continued) replaced the pan/zoom-anywhere SVG map with a fractal zoom-level ladder (system -> stage -> node), reached after a round of Artifact POCs tested the interaction model live before anything landed here — see docs/culture.md's 'prove the model before committing anything' precedent. Each level snaps the camera to its own full framing on entry (fitRectToView); exiting always steps back exactly one level, never straight to the top — that asymmetry was a real bug in the POCs (zooming out from the deepest level skipped the middle one) fixed by making the level ladder an explicit array instead of deriving level from a raw continuous scale number. A grown-radius focus mechanism opens a right-docked detail panel (a true CSS overlay drawer, not a reserved layout column — the map always measures the full viewport whether the panel is open or closed, fixed after Session 025 (continued) first shipped it as a static flex column that silently shrank the navigable canvas by 340px at all times). Reads/Writes/Loaded-by entries in the panel that name a real lattice node are clickable and travel the camera there (resolveRef/travelTo). Two lenses (Engineer / Auditor) recede off-lens nodes rather than hiding them, per the 'reveal state, never skip nor hide' principle from the Archives-visibility regression review. Screens (live pages with real screenshots) render as one more stage column, client-computed same as before — this file's only true hand-authored data. Adding a page, lattice node, or screenshot needs no edit here — only a rebuild of data/terrain-map.json.
loadedBy: manual browsing only — not embedded elsewhere yet

screenshot path convention changed: renderScreenPanel()'s image src construction here must match lib/build-terrain-map.js's findScreenshots() output
lib/build-terrain-map.js's stage/lens he: renderPanel()'s lens-tags block here reads engineerFocus/auditorFocus directly off each node — a renamed or reshaped field breaks it silently, no type check

lib/build-archives.js.role: generates pages/archives.html — the complete page registry: every arc's slugs as ported/missing cells, plus unsorted and dev/demo pages
lib/build-archives.js.context: Session 022 added an 'Unsorted (No Arc)' section: nodes.json entries with empty arcKeys are invisible in every arc section (those only ever iterate ARC_ORDER), which was a real gap for department-only content (phantom-opera-meta-review, vxg-thread-round-5) that has no arc membership yet. Sits alongside the pre-existing 'Dev & Demo Pages' section (viewmodels.json category:demo pages, which aren't nodes.json entries at all) as a second, distinct population of non-arc content — the two are kept separate rather than merged, since 'a real node with no arc yet' and 'a non-node dev fixture' are different states worth telling apart at a glance. Same session, later: also calls lib/auto-discover-nodes.js's discoverOrphanNodes() (same function lib/build-index.js calls) so a page merged with no nodes.json entry at all shows up in the Unsorted section automatically, not just as a PR-comment warning. This file reads nodes.json/arcs-v2.json directly rather than data/index.json — a pre-existing violation of 'everything downstream reads index.json' (see pe-011) that made this auto-discovery wiring a two-place job instead of one. Also Session 022: ARC_KEY_MAP's comment wrongly claimed data/arcs.json (the unrelated v1 file) uses kebab-case arc keys — it doesn't; both arcs files use snake_case, and the kebab-case format is purely the i18n string-key convention. Fixed the comment; see docs/architecture/03-data.md's 'v1 vs v2' section and config/lessons/v1-arcs-json-is-frozen-not-a-sync-target.json for the full v1/v2 relationship this traced.
reads: pages/*.html (existence check, per slug, for the ported/missing cell state; also read in full via lib/auto-discover-nodes.js for any slug with no nodes.json entry)

a node gets department/workType fields b: shows up automatically in the Unsorted section here — no build-archives.js change needed, renderUnsortedSection() reads arcKeys directly
ARC_ORDER or ARC_KEY_MAP changed: must stay in sync with data/arcs-v2.json's keys — a typo here silently drops that arc's whole section
auto-discovery logic changed: lib/build-index.js (calls the same function independently — see pe-011)

lib/check-design-tokens.js.role: non-blocking-in-spirit, blocking-in-practice check — every var(--token) reference in generated pages and stylesheets must resolve against a real token
lib/check-design-tokens.js.context: Built od-004's concrete deliverable: an automated replacement for remembering to check CSS token usage by hand. Two legitimate token families exist — the global light theme in styles/design-system.css, and a self-contained local dark :root block repeated across lib/build-archives.js, build-demo.js, build-specimens.js, and pages/specimen-architectural-wisdoms.html (see docs/architecture/12-design-system.md). A file's valid tokens are its own local :root tokens, plus the global set only if it actually links design-system.css — a fallback value inside var(--x, #hex) does not count as the token being defined, since that's exactly the pattern that partially masked the Session 015 bug.
reads: lib/build-*.js (every generator, scanned for inline <style> content)
pages/*.html (every static page, scanned for inline <style> content)
styles/*.css (every companion stylesheet, credited with global tokens unconditionally — see alwaysGlobal)
loadedBy: tests/13-check-design-tokens.test.js (also runs it as a CI-gating integration test)

a new local dark-theme file added: no action needed — checkFile() reads each file's own :root block dynamically, nothing to register
styles/design-system.css tokens added/re: every file linking it may now have new violations or newly-valid usages — re-run this script
a new companion stylesheet added under s: confirm it's actually always paired with design-system.css by its loader before relying on alwaysGlobal crediting it — if not, it needs its own local :root instead

lib/logger.js.role: structured logger — every build script's warn/info/error call goes through here
lib/logger.js.context: The widest fan-in of any file in lib/: nearly every build script imports { logger } and calls logger.warn({ code, message, ...fields }) rather than console.warn directly. Optional fields attach without changing the call signature — a call site can add { arc, slug } without touching any other call site. Swapping the backing store (e.g. write to a DB instead of console) means changing the three methods here, not every call site.
loadedBy: every other lib/build-*.js and lib/check-*.js script that logs a warning

logger method signature changed (warn/in: every call site across lib/ — all follow the same { code, message, ...fields } shape; a signature change ripples everywhere at once
backing store swapped (e.g. console → DB: no call site needs to change — that's the point of the single indirection point here

lib/logger-codes.js.role: event code registry — the CODES constant every logger.warn/error call references
lib/logger-codes.js.context: Adding a new logger call site means adding its code here first — grouped by the file that emits it, with a comment documenting the optional fields that call carries. Prevents the same failure mode magic-strings-to-constants.json (config/lessons/) already names for other string literals: a typo'd code string silently creates a new, uncorrelated event instead of erroring.

a new logger.warn/error call site added: add its CODES.* entry here first, grouped under a comment naming the emitting file
a CODES.* entry renamed or removed: grep the whole repo for the old name — nothing enforces this file and its call sites stay in sync automatically

lib/validate-blueprint.js.role: blueprint consistency validator — confirms every id in blueprint.json has a matching config file and vex-config constant
lib/validate-blueprint.js.context: blueprint.json declares which feature/category/assetType/template ids exist; config/{type}/{id}.json is expected to exist for each with required fields; vex-config.js's Feature/Category/AssetType constants are expected to cover every id too. This script is the only thing that actually checks all three agree — exits 1 and lists every mismatch if they don't.

a new id added to blueprint.json: config/{type}/{id}.json must exist with the required fields config/_meta.json declares for that type
lib/vex-config.js must have a matching constant (except templates, which are open-ended)
config/_meta.json required-field list ch: every existing config/{type}/{id}.json file must actually have those fields, or this script starts failing for files that were previously fine

lib/build-sitemap.js.role: generates sitemap.xml — lists only pages that actually exist, so crawlers never hit a 404
lib/build-sitemap.js.context: Filters nodes.json down to nodes with both a non-null id and an actual pages/{slug}.html file on disk before including them — unported nodes are silently excluded rather than producing a dead sitemap entry. UTILITY_PAGES is a small hardcoded list of non-node pages (archives, vextreme-demo, specimens) to include if they exist.

a new utility page added (non-node, not: UTILITY_PAGES array in this file — otherwise it's silently excluded from the sitemap

lib/strings-check.js.role: strings pipeline integrity check — runs before compile, catches missing EN text, stale translations, and orphaned keys
lib/strings-check.js.context: Four severity levels: BLOCK (missing EN text — compile cannot proceed, exits 1), REMAP (key found in migrations.json — auto-remaps in source), WARN (EN text changed since last compile — tags existing translations _stale), INFO (orphaned key whose EN text hash matches another key — suggests a missing migration entry). Unmatched orphans get quarantined to data/strings/orphans.json rather than silently dropped. Mutates source files in place for REMAP and WARN cases — this is the one script in the strings pipeline that writes back into data/strings/source/, not just reads it.
writes: data/strings/source/**/*.json (in-place mutation: remap/delete keys, tag stale translations)
loadedBy: manual run: node lib/strings-check.js (intended to run before lib/strings-compile.js)

manifest schema changed (enHash field): the WARN staleness check here reads manifest[key].enHash directly
migrations.json schema changed: loadMigrations() here — byFrom/byTo lookup shape must match

lib/build-index-page.js.role: generates index.html — the root landing page, arc hub links plus the 5 most recently added nodes
lib/build-index-page.js.context: The first page a visitor or crawler hits. Reads nodes.json directly for recency (by id, not date) rather than going through data/index.json's arcMap, since 'most recently added' is a raw-nodes-order question, not an arc-position question. Has its own local :root light-theme token block (see docs/architecture/12-design-system.md) — a renamed restatement of styles/design-system.css's tokens, not a copy of the dashboard dark theme; noted there as a minor non-urgent migration opportunity, not tracked as debt.

local :root token values changed: consider migrating to styles/design-system.css's shared tokens instead, per docs/architecture/12-design-system.md — this file's local block is the last one of its kind
'most recent 5 nodes' selection logic ch: confirm it still reads by nodes.json id order, not data/index.json's arc-derived order — they answer different questions

lib/session-bootstrap.js.role: one-shot session-start state report — read-only, gathers what would otherwise be several manual checks into one command
lib/session-bootstrap.js.context: Written directly in response to a standing observation recorded in docs/culture.md ('What an AI instance actually needs here'): reconstructing 'what's the current state' at the start of a session meant reading INDEX.json, running git log, and running three separate verification commands by hand, every time. Not a replacement for the continuity system — this is the fast mechanical glance (is anything broken, what changed, how many open items); the reasoning behind any of it still lives in the continuity log. Guards against a real recursion hazard: it runs the full test suite internally, which includes its own test file (tests/14), whose integration tests would otherwise spawn this script again — VEX_BOOTSTRAP_NESTED=1 tells that nested run to skip instead of recursing.
reads: docs/continuity/batch-00N/ directory listing (newest YYYY-MM-DD-session-0NN.md file, when the active batch is a directory)
node --test tests/*.test.js (via subprocess, with VEX_BOOTSTRAP_NESTED=1 to prevent recursion)

docs/continuity/INDEX.md's 'Last updated: parseLastSession() / parseBatchRegistry() here — both parse that exact markdown shape by regex
data/status.json's notices schema change: summarizeStatus() here reads status.notices generically, but confirm the shape still matches
a new tests/*.test.js file added that it: it must check process.env.VEX_BOOTSTRAP_NESTED and skip its own integration test when nested, same pattern as tests/14 — otherwise the same recursion hazard recurs for a new file
the per-session filename convention (YYY: latestSessionFile() here — filters and sorts by that exact filename shape; docs/continuity/batch-003/README.md and INDEX.md's batch rules document the convention it assumes
lib/check-map-bindings.js — enforces the same convention against the registry's declared range

lib/append-session-continuation.js.role: append-only continuity helper — appends a same-session continuation block at EOF without treating the VXG marker as an insertion anchor
lib/append-session-continuation.js.context: Built after Codex nearly repeated Session 023's continuity-placement failure shape while appending a Session 024 continuation: repeated VXG markers and display-path encoding made text-patch anchors brittle. This helper encodes the local rule physically: a continuation starts with '### Session continued', the target session file must already end with the VXG marker, line endings are preserved, and the new block is appended after EOF rather than inserted before any marker. The VXG marker remains a completion boundary/signature, not a search target.

lib/check-map-bindings.js.role: pe-013 layered-maps drift detector — verifies the bindings between cold-start maps and the files they claim to bind
lib/check-map-bindings.js.context: Built in Session 024 after Session 023's continuity entries were injected into the wrong session record and nothing mechanical noticed: INDEX.md claimed one latest session while the record held another. The layered-maps culture (docs/culture.md) says every new context layer needs the health check that would detect its map drifting; this is that check for the continuity layer. Checks: CLAUDE.md → CONTEXT-NOTES.md pointer; CONTEXT-NOTES.md Notes table ↔ context-notes/*.md both directions; Batch Registry rows exist, exactly one active row, last, agreeing with the Active batch line; directory-form batch has README.md and convention-named session files inside the declared range; INDEX.md's 'Last updated: Session NNN' equals the newest session file on disk. Any finding exits 1 — drift posture like build-lattice-headers --check, not informational like check-key-alignment.
reads: docs/continuity/INDEX.md (batch registry, Active batch line, Last updated line)
writes: stdout only — read-only, changes nothing; exit code 1 on drift

INDEX.md's Batch Registry table, Active: parseActiveBatchLine()/sessionRange() here, and parseBatchRegistry()/parseLastSession() in lib/session-bootstrap.js — all parse those exact markdown shapes by regex
CONTEXT-NOTES.md's Notes table stops usi: parseNoteRows()/checkContextNoteBindings() here — the path regex and the README.md exemption encode those two assumptions
a new map layer is added (a folder READM: add its binding check here — the layered-maps culture section says the map and its health check arrive together

lib/check-lattice-edges.js.role: pe-012 lattice-edge drift detector — verifies the map's claimed reads/writes/loadedBy edges against actual code
lib/check-lattice-edges.js.context: Built in Session 024 (continued). The lattice's edges are hand-written prose; Session 022 found two stale claims by hand, and docs/architecture/13-intent-driven-operations.md named this checker as the prerequisite for trusting the map in higher-blast-radius automation (od-008). Three verifications: claimed read paths exist on disk; the node's own source (LATTICE header stripped, so a claim cannot vouch for itself) mentions each claimed target's basename; claimed loaders mention the node (or a mediating artifact named in the edge's annotation), and every actual require() of a mapped node appears in that node's loadedBy. First real run found 22 stale/missing edges, all fixed the same session. Informational at the CLI (mention heuristics have false negatives); tests/23's integration test is what makes stale edges fail CI. Its own development hit the sentinel hazard twice: a comment spelling out a require, then a test fixture naming a mapped node — comments are stripped, fixtures name unmapped files.
reads: lib/, tests/, widgets/, components/ *.js sources (mention + require() scans)
any file a node names in reads/writes/loadedBy (existence + mention checks)
writes: stdout only — read-only; exit 1 only with --strict (not wired into CI)

docs/lattice-map.json edge prose convent: pathToken()/isTemplate()/mentionName() here — they encode exactly those conventions; a new prose shape silently becomes a skipped edge, not an error
a loadedBy edge describes an indirect co: the edge's annotation must name the mediating artifact path (e.g. 'pages/ecosystem-hub.html (live fetch of its output data/status.json)') — that is what the annotation fallback checks
a new lib/tests/widgets file mentions a: comments are stripped by requiresOf(); string fixtures are not — use unmapped fixture paths in tests, per tests/23's own header note

lib/trace-string-usage.js.role: string reverse-tracer — data-i18n usage in pages/wip HTML cross-referenced against the compiled manifest
lib/trace-string-usage.js.context: Built in Session 025 alongside the victor_dossier merge, answering the 'where is this text used?' / 'what does this page use?' questions structurally rather than by tribal memory — the same capability the wip/ registry-graph context notes named (getBindingsForString, getUIElementsByContext), scoped to what's actually validatable today: a grep of data-i18n attributes against data/strings/compiled/manifest.json's per-key language list. Three modes: --key, --page, and a repo-wide orphan/unused summary. Read-only, informational — a key with zero page usage is often legitimate work in progress, not dead weight.

blueprint.json.role: system blueprint — canonical registry of all feature IDs, category IDs, template IDs
blueprint.json.context: The configuration contract. Every Feature constant in vex-config.js must have a matching entry here, and every entry here must have a config file in config/features/. Tests/06 enforces this. Adding a new feature to FEATURES registry in build-vextreme.js without adding it here will fail the blueprint tests.
